options.text: Write a message about schedule changes
I need to write two messages about our team meetings moving from Monday mornings to Wednesday afternoons - one casual message for my team and one professional email for the department

I need to communicate a schedule change where our team meetings are moving from Monday mornings to Wednesday afternoons starting next month. Please write two versions: 1) A casual Slack message for my immediate team members who attend these meetings regularly, and 2) A professional email for the broader department who may be affected by our availability changes. The Slack message should be brief and conversational, while the email should be more formal with clear subject line and proper context about why this affects them.

responses.response_weak: Hi everyone, there are some schedule changes coming up. Meetings will be moving to a new time. Please check the updated calendar and adjust your schedules accordingly. Let me know if you have questions. Thanks.
responses.response_medium:
Here are two versions of the schedule change message:

Casual version for your team:
Hey team, quick heads up — our Monday morning meetings are moving to Wednesday afternoons starting next month. I will send updated calendar invites soon. Let me know if you have any conflicts!

Professional version for the department:
Dear colleagues, I wanted to let you know that our team meetings will be moving from Monday mornings to Wednesday afternoons beginning next month. This may affect our availability during that time slot. Please keep this in mind for any scheduling needs. Thank you for your flexibility, and feel free to reach out with any questions.
responses.response_strong:
## 1. Casual Slack Message (for your team)

Hey team! Heads up — starting next month, our weekly meeting is moving from Monday mornings to **Wednesday afternoons**. I know Mondays were rough anyway, so hopefully this is a welcome change.

I will send updated calendar invites this week. If Wednesday afternoons are a problem for anyone, ping me and we will figure it out.

## 2. Professional Department Email

**Subject:** Schedule Change: [Team Name] Weekly Meeting Moving to Wednesdays

Good afternoon,

I wanted to let you know about an upcoming change to our team's meeting schedule that may affect cross-team coordination.

**What is changing:**
Our weekly team meeting is moving from Monday mornings to Wednesday afternoons, effective [start date].

**How this may affect you:**
During our Wednesday afternoon meetings, our team's availability for ad hoc requests, collaborative sessions, and cross-departmental meetings will be limited. If you currently rely on us during that window, we would be happy to work with you on alternative timing.

**What to do:**
- If you have standing meetings with our team on Wednesday afternoons, please reach out so we can reschedule
- For urgent requests during our meeting time, [contact name/channel] will be your best point of contact

Thank you for your understanding. Please do not hesitate to reach out if you have any questions about how this change affects your workflow.

Best regards,
[Your Name]

feedback.what_happened: The weak prompt gives the AI almost nothing to work with, so it creates a generic, unhelpful message that doesn't match either audience or include key details. The medium prompt specifies the two audiences and basic facts, so the AI produces appropriate messages for each context, but they're still pretty bare-bones. The effective prompt gives the AI everything it needs — the specific change, why each audience cares, what tone to use, and what elements to include — so it creates polished, complete messages that actually solve the communication challenge.
feedback.principle: Give the AI specific context about your audience, purpose, and desired outcome so it can tailor the response appropriately.
feedback.tip: When you need different versions for different audiences, tell the AI who each audience is and why they care about your message — this helps it adjust tone, detail level, and focus for each group.
